description: Keep a bridge only if at least one cross-cluster premise has in-degree <= theta (a specialized lemma, not a high-in-degree utility hub); choose theta by the elbow/kneedle of the bridge-count vs theta curve on the power-law in-degree distribution.
motivation: Filter out 'utility connections' (foundational lemmas used everywhere) to isolate genuine bridges.
formal_results: In-degree sweep: theta=1->15, theta=2->34, theta=3->60; the elbow selects theta=2 -> 34 filtered bridge theorems.
results_description: Requiring a specialized (low-in-degree) cross-cluster premise collapses 280 raw bridges to 34.
conclusions: An in-degree depth filter (theta=2, chosen by elbow) isolates 34 genuine operational bridges from the 280 raw candidates.
